heading: Naturalization authority
label: 1421 Naturalization authority
notes: Amendments1994—Subsec. (b)(5)(A). substituted “district court” for “District Court”.Pub. L. 103–4161991—Subsec. (b). , amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “An applicant for naturalization may choose to have the oath of allegiance under administered by the Attorney General or by any district court of the United States for any State or by any court of record in any State having a seal, a clerk, and jurisdiction in actions in law or equity, or law and equity, in which the amount in controversy is unlimited. The jurisdiction of all courts in this subsection specified to administer the oath of allegiance shall extend only to persons resident within the respective jurisdiction of such courts.”Pub. L. 102–232, § 102(a)section 1448(a) of this title, substituted “district court” for “District Court”.Pub. L. 102–232, § 305(a)1990— amended section generally, substituting provisions authorizing Attorney General to naturalize persons as citizens, for provisions granting certain courts exclusive jurisdiction to naturalize.Pub. L. 101–6491988—Subsec. (e). struck out subsec. (e) which read as follows: “Notwithstanding the provisions of section 405(a), any petition for naturalization filed on or after , shall be heard and determined in accordance with the requirements of this subchapter.”Pub. L. 100–525September 26, 19611961—Subsec. (e). added subsec. (e).Pub. L. 87–3011959—Subsec. (a). struck out provisions which conferred jurisdiction on District Court for Territory of Hawaii. See , Judiciary and Judicial Procedure, and notes thereunder.Pub. L. 86–3section 91 of Title 281958—Subsec. (a). struck out provisions which conferred jurisdiction on District Court for Territory of Alaska. See , which established a United States District Court for the State of Alaska.Pub. L. 85–508section 81A of Title 28

Effective Date of 1990 Amendment; Savings Provision, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–649, title IV, § 408104 Stat. 5047Pub. L. 102–232, title III, § 305(n)105 Stat. 1750Nov. 29, 1990Dec. 12, 1991“(a)Effective Date.—“(1).—No new court petitions after effective dateNo court shall have jurisdiction, under section 310(a) of the Immigration and Nationality Act [], to naturalize a person unless a petition for naturalization with respect to that person has been filed with the court before .8 U.S.C. 1421(a)October 1, 1991“(2)Treatment of current court petitions.—“(A).—Continuation of current rulesExcept as provided in subparagraph (B), any petition for naturalization which may be pending in a court on , shall be heard and determined in accordance with the requirements of law in effect when the petition was filed.October 1, 1991“(B).—Permitting withdrawal and consideration of application under new rulesIn the case of any petition for naturalization which may be pending in any court on , the petitioner may withdraw such petition and have the petitioner’s application for naturalization considered under the amendments made by this title [amending this section, sections 1101, 1423, 1424, 1426 to 1430, 1433, 1435 to 1440, 1441 to 1451, and 1455 of this title, and , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and repealing ], but only if the petition is withdrawn not later than 3 months after the effective date.January 1, 1992section 1429 of Title 18section 1459 of this title“(3).—General effective dateExcept as otherwise provided in this section, the amendments made by this title are effective as of the date of the enactment of this Act [].Nov. 29, 1990“(b).—Interim, Final RegulationsThe Attorney General shall prescribe regulations (on an interim, final basis or otherwise) to implement the amendments made by this title on a timely basis.“(c).—Continuing DutiesThe amendments to section 339 of the Immigration and Nationality Act [] (relating to functions and duties of clerks) shall not apply to functions and duties respecting petitions filed before .8 U.S.C. 1450October 1, 1991“(d).—General Savings Provisions(1)Nothing contained in this title [amending this section, sections 1101, 1423, 1424, 1426 to 1430, 1433, 1435 to 1440, 1441 to 1451, and 1455 of this title, and , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, repealing , and enacting provisions set out as a note under ], unless otherwise specifically provided, shall be construed to affect the validity of any declaration of intention, petition for naturalization, certificate of naturalization, certification of citizenship, or other document or proceeding which is valid as of the effective date; or to affect any prosecution, suit, action, or proceedings, civil or criminal, brought, or any status, condition, right in process of acquisition, act, thing, liability, obligation, or matter, civil or criminal, done or existing, as of the effective date.section 1429 of Title 18section 1459 of this titlesection 1440 of this title“(2)As to all such prosecutions, suits, actions, proceedings, statutes, conditions, rights, acts, things, liabilities, obligations, or matters, the provisions of law repealed by this title are, unless otherwise specifically provided, hereby continued in force and effect.“(e).—Treatment of Service in Armed Forces of Foreign CountryThe amendments made by section 404 [amending ] (relating to treatment of service in armed forces of a foreign country) shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [] and shall apply to exemptions from training or service obtained before, on, or after such date.section 1426 of this titleNov. 29, 1990“(f).—Filipino War VeteransSection 405 [enacting provisions formerly set out as a note under ] (relating to naturalization of natives of the Philippines through active-duty service under United States command during World War II) shall become effective on , without regard to whether regulations to implement such section have been issued by such date.”section 1440 of this titleMay 1, 1991

children.heading: Authority in Attorney General
children.label: a Authority in Attorney General
children.text: The sole authority to naturalize persons as citizens of the United States is conferred upon the Attorney General.

children.heading: Court authority to administer oaths
children.label: b Court authority to administer oaths

children.heading: General jurisdiction
children.label: A General jurisdiction
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), each applicant for naturalization may choose to have the oath of allegiance under administered by the Attorney General or by an eligible court described in paragraph (5). Each such eligible court shall have authority to administer such oath of allegiance to persons residing within the jurisdiction of the court.section 1448(a) of this title

children.heading: Exclusive authority
children.label: B Exclusive authority
children.text: An eligible court described in paragraph (5) that wishes to have exclusive authority to administer the oath of allegiance under to persons residing within the jurisdiction of the court during the period described in paragraph (3)(A)(i) shall notify the Attorney General of such wish and, subject to this subsection, shall have such exclusive authority with respect to such persons during such period.section 1448(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the applicant for naturalization shall notify the Attorney General of the intent to be naturalized before the court, and

children.label: I
children.text: shall forward to the court (not later than 10 days after the date of approval of an application for naturalization in the case of a court which has provided notice under paragraph (1)(B)) such information as may be necessary to administer the oath of allegiance under , andsection 1448(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: shall promptly forward to the court a certificate of naturalization (prepared by the Attorney General).

children.heading: Assignment of individuals in the case of exclusive authority
children.label: B Assignment of individuals in the case of exclusive authority
i
children.text: the court’s exclusive authority to administer the oath of allegiance under to such a person during the period specified in paragraph (3)(A)(i), andsection 1448(a) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the date or dates (if any) under paragraph (3)(B) on which the court has scheduled oath administration ceremonies.

children.heading: Limited period and advance notice required
children.label: A Limited period and advance notice required
i
children.text: only during the 45-day period beginning on the date on which the Attorney General certifies to the court that an applicant is eligible for naturalization, and

children.label: ii
children.text: only if the court has notified the Attorney General, prior to the date of certification of eligibility, of the day or days (during such 45-day period) on which the court has scheduled oath administration ceremonies.

children.heading: Authority of Attorney General
children.label: B Authority of Attorney General
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (C), the Attorney General shall not administer the oath of allegiance to a person under subsection (a) during the period in which exclusive authority to administer the oath of allegiance may be exercised by an eligible court under this subsection with respect to that person.

children.heading: Waiver of exclusive authority
children.label: C Waiver of exclusive authority
children.text: Notwithstanding the previous provisions of this paragraph, a court may waive exclusive authority to administer the oath of allegiance under to a person under this subsection if the Attorney General has not provided the court with the certification described in subparagraph (A)(i) within a reasonable time before the date scheduled by the court for oath administration ceremonies. Upon notification of a court’s waiver of jurisdiction, the Attorney General shall promptly notify the applicant.section 1448(a) of this title

children.heading: Issuance of certificates
children.label: 4 Issuance of certificates
children.text: The Attorney General shall provide for the issuance of certificates of naturalization at the time of administration of the oath of allegiance.

children.label: A
children.text: a district court of the United States in any State, or

children.label: B
children.text: any court of record in any State having a seal, a clerk, and jurisdiction in actions in law or equity, or law and equity, in which the amount in controversy is unlimited.

children.heading: Judicial review
children.label: c Judicial review
children.text: A person whose application for naturalization under this subchapter is denied, after a hearing before an immigration officer under , may seek review of such denial before the United States district court for the district in which such person resides in accordance with chapter 7 of title 5. Such review shall be de novo, and the court shall make its own findings of fact and conclusions of law and shall, at the request of the petitioner, conduct a hearing de novo on the application.section 1447(a) of this title

children.heading: Sole procedure
children.label: d Sole procedure
children.text: A person may only be naturalized as a citizen of the United States in the manner and under the conditions prescribed in this subchapter and not otherwise.
